code.parse_mode_reason: Beller2020Language instruction video is a 2D cartoon physics clip; 3D lifting is forbidden.

attached_instructions.text: In this experiment, your task is to choose a description that best captures what happened in a video clip. We will show you clips in which physical objects interact. All of the clips will take place in the rectangular space shown below. Objects will either start on screen or enter from the right. Objects can exit the space through the red exit on the left but only when it is not blocked by the brown gate . The position of the brown gate can be changed by pressing either of the green buttons next to it. In each clip you will see some of the following four objects: a green ball (labeled with A ) a grey ball (labeled with B ) an unlabeled blue ball a box Not all of the objects will appear in every clip, however every clip will contain ball A and ball B . In all of the clips, ball B will go through the red exit . Below is an example clip. In the experiment, you will view each clip at least twice. You may replay the clip as many times as you like. After viewing a clip, choose which description best captures the clip you watched. The options are: Ball A caused Ball B to go through the gate. Ball A enabled Ball B to go through the gate. Ball A affected Ball B's going through the gate. Ball A made no difference to Ball B's going through the gate.
